summary.intent: Fix accordion component controlled prop sync issues identified by Codex review on PR #662 in the fogodev/ars-ui repository, specifically around Bindable transitions and normalization when multiple mode changes.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented and merged fixes for controlled-mode entry/exit state machine transitions and single-mode normalization. All CI checks passed (formatting, Clippy, Workspace Check, Unit Tests), mutation testing completed cleanly (155 caught, 0 missed), and the fix was committed and pushed.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot reviewer that reacts with 'eyes' while reviewing and posts P1-severity threads when it finds issues; the review loop is: push → wait for Codex reaction → handle threads → push again.
The project uses cargo-mutants for mutation testing on state machine code; a clean pass is 155 caught / 36 unviable / 0 missed for the accordion module.
cargo llvm-cov is used for coverage gating; accordion/mod.rs maintained 97.52% line coverage after the fix.
spec/components/navigation/accordion.md is a normative spec that must be kept in sync with implementation changes; cargo xtask spec validate enforces this.
The accordion component uses a SyncControlledValue(BTreeSet<Key>) event for controlled prop synchronization, with None→Some emitting SyncControlledValue and Some→None emitting SyncProps.
SyncProps must sync controlled mode from current props before normalization runs.

code.finding: on_props_changed handles controlled Bindable transitions; the bug was that None→Some and Some→None paths were not emitting the correct sync events, leaving state machine out of sync with controlled value.
SyncProps handler needed to read current props to re-establish controlled mode before running normalization, especially when multiple transitions from true to false.

workflow: gh pr checks can return stale status; gh run view with --json is more reliable for checking individual job completion.
The correct gate sequence before pushing a state-machine fix is: unit tests → snapshot tests → spec validation → clippy → coverage → mutation testing.
Regression tests should be written before patching the implementation to confirm the failing behavior matches the reported issue.
cargo-mutants on the accordion module takes significant wall time (multiple polling cycles); do not interrupt it for state-machine changes.
After a formatting-only commit, triggering Codex review again is necessary because Codex reviews the new HEAD, not just semantic changes.

friction: gh pr checks returned stale/incorrect status requiring fallback to gh run view for accurate CI state.
cargo-mutants ran for an extended period with no intermediate output, requiring many polling cycles before producing a final summary.
Codex review latency required multiple sleep-and-poll cycles after each push before new threads appeared.
Two separate push/review cycles were needed: one for a formatting-only fix, then another for the substantive controlled sync fix.
open_items: Codex may open additional review threads on the controlled sync fix in its next pass; those would require another fix-and-push iteration.
The SyncProps normalization behavior on multiple=false transitions was underdocumented in the spec and was only updated as part of this fix — worth a broader spec audit for similar edge cases.
The tabs component (crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/mod.rs) has similar SyncControlledValue patterns that may have the same controlled entry/exit bugs — not investigated in this session.
